name: Range v. Attorney General
summary: The Third Circuit, sitting en banc, held that the federal felon-in-possession statute was unconstitutional as applied to a person convicted of a non-violent, non-serious offense, finding insufficient historical support for permanently disarming all convicted felons.
significance: The Third Circuit's en banc decision in Range — holding that a non-violent felon retained Second Amendment rights — is frequently cited to show that the historical tradition test can limit categorical firearms bans, supporting the respondent's argument that marijuana users cannot be permanently disarmed by status alone.
